Patch Set 4:

Sorry for what may be a foolish question, but is this intended as a replacement for or a complement to Jinshan's patch 'http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/13138/'?

Also, what benefit, specifically, do we derive from using IOC_MDC_GETFILEINFO instead of fstatat?

Patch Set 4:

Patrick, I think the two patches are complimentary.

This patch avoids getting the layout lock when fetching the file attributes, by using IOC_MDC_GETFILEINFO instead of fstatat() that fetches the layout lock. While in many cases using the attributes from only the MDT would be inaccurate, the MDT attributes are authoritative for a released file and that is the only case that matters here. 

Jinshan's patch is also fixing a bug on the MDS about lock revocation, so that when the file is being restored the right lock is being held.

Patch Set 4:

> Patrick, I think the two patches are complimentary.

I think that this patch should be used and Jinshan's should not.

> This patch avoids getting the layout lock when fetching the file attributes, by using IOC_MDC_GETFILEINFO instead of fstatat() that fetches the layout lock. While in many cases using the attributes from only the MDT would be inaccurate, the MDT attributes are authoritative for a released file and that is the only case that matters here.

> Jinshan's patch is also fixing a bug on the MDS about lock revocation, so that when the file is being restored the right lock is being held.

Why do we need to revoke that lock except to update LLIF_FILE_RESTORING which only exists to avoid the deadlock that is avoided by this patch? The less done in the already too complicated HSM restore path the better.

Patch Set 5:

John, should this (or a follow-on) patch be removing LLIF_FILE_RESTORING, or is that needed for compatibility with other copytools that do not have this patch?  If it cannot be removed immediately, does it make sense to schedule it for deletion under:

 #ifdef LUSTRE_VERSION_CODE < OBD_OCD_VERSION(3, 0, 53, 0)

or similar?

Patch Set 5:

> John, should this (or a follow-on) patch be removing LLIF_FILE_RESTORING, or is that needed for compatibility with other copytools that do not have this patch?

I think we can remove it and I plan to do so in a follow on patch. I have not yet fully checked that it is no longer needed. Since the change is inside of the HSM library there are no compatibility issues, so long as the other copytools are following the rules.

parents.subject: LU-6049 obdclass: Add synchro in lu_context_key_degister()

commit.subject: LU-4727 hsm: use IOC_MDC_GETFILEINFO in restore
commit.message:
LU-4727 hsm: use IOC_MDC_GETFILEINFO in restore

Use IOC_MDC_GETFILEINFO rather than fstatat() to get the original file
attributes during restore. Add test_12p to sanity-hsm to check that
triggering an implicit restore from the copytool's own mount point
does not wedge the copytool.

Signed-off-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Change-Id: I1b1eeb703c60907a2759fdb6d8fb8728a13f8918
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/13750
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>

Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
